title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Ryan Gosling hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling hosted 'Saturday Night Live' on March 7, marking his fourth time in the role.
Musical guest Gorillaz celebrated their 25th anniversary by performing 'Clint Eastwood' on the show.
Next week's 'SNL' features Harry Styles as host and musical guest, following his appearance in the audience.

title: Crew member on sunken vessel off Cape Cod has died, officials identify him
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Angel Nieves, 37, of New Bedford, died after the Yankee Rose capsized off Cape Cod, officials confirmed.
The search for the second crew member of the Yankee Rose was suspended due to adverse weather conditions.
The Yankee Rose had a previous incident on February 26 involving carbon monoxide exposure among eight people.

title: Patriots reportedly among teams interested in Packers’ leading WR in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are closely monitoring Packers wide receiver Romeo Doubs, who is a free agent target this offseason.
Romeo Doubs, turning 26 in April, has had at least 600 receiving yards each season for the past three years.
Doubs is projected to receive a three-year, $49 million contract, with $30 million guaranteed, in free agency.

title: NH cold case solved 40 years after police found man’s skull in woods
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The remains found in Bristol, NH woods in 1986 have been identified as Warren Kuchinsky, born in 1952.
The DNA Doe Project used genetic genealogy to confirm Kuchinsky's identity through a family member's DNA.
No foul play is suspected in Kuchinsky’s death, last known to be alive in the mid-1970s.

title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police warn of a scam involving fake parking violation notices falsely appearing from Boston Municipal Court.
The fraudulent notices feature court details and a Massachusetts state seal, misleading recipients to a scam QR code.
Recipients are urged to verify notices through the city's website and report fraudulent ones to CrimeStoppers.

title: Mansfield man charged with illegally possessing ammunition, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Michael Kennedy, 48, of Mansfield, charged with illegally possessing ammunition as a convicted felon, federal prosecutors say.
Authorities found a loaded 5.7-caliber pistol and firearm parts in Kennedy's home, including components for M-16- and AR-15-style rifles.
Kennedy faces up to 15 years in prison if convicted, with prior convictions for bank robbery and related felonies.

title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward showcases speed with 12 stolen bases in 12 games, leading all Red Sox players in spring training.
Max Ferguson impresses with a grand slam and 7 RBIs, offering positional versatility for potential call-up.
Tayron Guerrero demonstrates reliability in bullpen, allowing one earned run in six innings with six strikeouts.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson is second in 5-on-5 goals for the Bruins this season with 13 goals.
Coach Marco Sturm confirmed Arvidsson will remain with the Bruins despite him being a pending free agent.
Arvidsson's potential contract extension is undecided, but he could be a valuable bridge player for the Bruins.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots traded center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears, prompting a potential reshuffle of their offensive line.
With Jared Wilson possibly moving to center, the Patriots are expected to seek a new left guard in the offseason.
Potential guard options include free agents Isaac Seumalo, David Edwards, Joel Bitonio, and draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Make-A-Wish Foundation.
Perry is inspired by the children in the Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island region served by Make-A-Wish.
He has supported Make-A-Wish for 15 years, motivated by the spirit and resilience of the children involved.
